setting.note: Camus's Algiers is a city of white terraces, harbour light and Sunday tedium, where an ordinary clerk drifts from a funeral toward a killing on the sand. The map runs west from the courthouse and prison of the old European quarter out past the beaches beyond the bay to the inland town where the novel opens.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Albert Camus, first published 1942. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Stranger, Albert Camus (1942). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The blue of the Mediterranean and the indifferent sky that closes the book. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
